displayTitle: One Wall or Two? Picking the Pipe Shot

beats.narration: Every pipe shot starts with two questions: how many walls does the beam cross, and how many do you read?

payload.title: ONE WALL OR TWO?
payload.sub: Count the walls the beam crosses — and the walls you read.

beats.narration: Three answers: single wall, cross one, view one. Double wall, single view: read the film side. Double wall, double view: read both.
Closed pipe above 3.5 inches: source offset outside one wall, film opposite. Read only the sharp film-side weld. Three shots minimum, 120 degrees apart.

payload.heading: DWSI: closed pipe over 3.5 in. OD

beats.narration: 3.5 inches and under, read both walls. Offset the source along the axis; the weld opens into an ellipse. Two shots, 90 degrees apart.

payload.heading: DWDI elliptical: 3.5 in. OD and under

beats.narration: So the rule: single wall when you can, double wall only when access forces it. Plan the shots, and the films tile the weld.

payload.sub: Double wall only when access forces it.
